band_rationale: High: a genuinely good spec that deliberately contains trigger-adjacent wording in benign contexts ('simple'/'quick-add'/'faster' in Out of Scope, 'latency' with a threshold, 'scalable' bounded to 250k rows/day). Ceiling 100 so that FIXING the labeled false positive passes the gate; floor 90 tolerates only that one labeled FP (94) — a second unlabeled finding also trips the unlabeled-unexpected gate. Engine output at labeling time: 94.
note: Precision guard (#53b/#56): prose that superficially resembles trigger words but is fine. SPEC-AMBIGUOUS-WORDING fires on 'scalable' in FR-003 even though the requirement is fully quantified (250,000 rows/day, 10-minute window) — a labeled known false positive: it counts in the precision denominator but not the numerator, keeping the metric honest while the gate passes.
